meaning: n.野鹰adj.憔悴的,野性的,形容枯槁的
examples: She was looking very haggard and worn.
He was pale and a bit haggard.
The poor fellow's face was haggard, and his eyes showed the fear that was upon him.
The haggard appearance that Malskat had acquired by living on park benches belied his prestigious background.
In one of its current spots a haggard-looking single mom argues with a deadbeat dad who's moving out-of-state.
